class.name: AudioStreamPlayer
class.description: [AudioStreamPlayer] plays an audio stream. The player's volume and panning are independent on its position on the screen, that is, audio plays as if it was coming from the center of the screen, and close to the listener, with no panning or filtering applied by default. It is ideal for user interfaces and background music. The audio to be played must be set in [member stream]. The stream must contain valid stored or fed audio data, for example, an [AudioStreamWAV] containing data from a [code].wav[/code] file, or an [AudioStreamMicrophone] receiving data from an input device. To play audio dependent on the position of the node, use [AudioStreamPlayer2D] or [AudioStreamPlayer3D] instead. To handle playback of an audio stream resource at a low level, use the [AudioStreamPlayback] class. To understand how audio files are imported, read [ResourceImporterWAV], [ResourceImporterOggVorbis], and [ResourceImporterMP3]. [b]Note:[/b] Hiding an [AudioStreamPlayer] node does not mute it. To mute the player, set [member volume_linear] to [code]0.0[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] While [AudioStreamPlayer] can only hold one [AudioStream], some streams can hold and play multiple sub-streams, like [AudioStreamInteractive]. [b]Note:[/b] Loading [AudioStreamOggVorbis], [AudioStreamMP3], or [AudioStreamInteractive] as stream adds an additional player parameter in the inspector. For OggVorbis and MP3 streams, that parameter is the loop override. If you enable [code]Looping[/code], then its associated boolean will be checked for loops, instead of the [code]Loop[/code] parameter from Import. For [AudioStreamInteractive], the parameter is used to switch clips from the editor, and behaves like [method AudioStreamPlaybackInteractive.switch_to_clip_by_name].

method.name: get_playback_position
method.description: Returns the playback position in the latest [AudioStream] played, in seconds. Returns [code]0.0[/code] if the stream is not playing. [b]Note:[/b] The playback position is not always accurate, as the [AudioServer] does not mix audio every processed frame. To get more accurate results, add [method AudioServer.get_time_since_last_mix] to the returned playback position. [b]Note:[/b] This method always returns [code]0.0[/code] if the [member stream] is [AudioStreamInteractive], [AudioStreamMicrophone], or [AudioStreamPolyphonic].

method.name: get_stream_playback
method.description: Returns the latest [AudioStreamPlayback] of this node, usually the most recently created by [method play]. If the stream is not playing, this method fails and returns an empty playback.

method.name: has_stream_playback
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the stream is active, even if [member stream_paused] is set to [code]true[/code]. See [member playing] and [method get_stream_playback].

method.name: play
method.description: Plays the stream from the beginning, or the given playback position, in seconds. Negative values and values higher than the total length of the stream are not accepted. [b]Note:[/b] [AudioStreamGenerator], [AudioStreamInteractive], [AudioStreamMicrophone], [AudioStreamPolyphonic], and [AudioStreamWAV] using [constant AudioStreamWAV.FORMAT_IMA_ADPCM] ignore [param from_position].

method.name: seek
method.description: Sets the playback position of the stream [param to_position], in seconds. Does nothing if the stream is not playing. Negative values and values higher than the total length of the stream are not accepted. [b]Note:[/b] [AudioStreamGenerator], [AudioStreamInteractive], [AudioStreamMicrophone], [AudioStreamPolyphonic], and [AudioStreamWAV] using [constant AudioStreamWAV.FORMAT_IMA_ADPCM] do not support seeking.

members.member: If [code]true[/code], this node calls [method play] when entering the tree.

The target bus name. The stream from this node will be routed to this bus. [b]Note:[/b] At runtime, if no bus with the given name exists, the stream will fall back on [code]"Master"[/code]. See also [method AudioServer.get_bus_name].
The maximum number of playbacks this node can hold at the same time. Calling [method play] after this value is reached will cut off the oldest playbacks.
The mix target channels. Has no effect when two speakers or less are detected (see [enum AudioServer.SpeakerMode]).
The audio's pitch and tempo, as a multiplier of the [member stream]'s sample rate. A value of [code]2.0[/code] doubles the audio's pitch, while a value of [code]0.5[/code] halves the pitch.
The playback type of the stream player. If set other than to the default value, it will force that playback type.
If [code]true[/code], this node is playing the stream. Setting this property has the same effect as [method play] and [method stop].
The [AudioStream] resource to be played. Setting this property stops the currently playing stream. If left empty, the [AudioStreamPlayer] does not work.

If [code]true[/code], the stream is paused. Setting [member stream_paused] to [code]false[/code] resumes the stream. [b]Note:[/b] This property is automatically changed when exiting or entering the tree, or this node is paused (see [member Node.process_mode]).

Volume of the stream, in decibels. This is an offset of the [member stream]'s volume. [b]Tip:[/b] To convert between decibel and linear energy (like most volume sliders do), use [method @GlobalScope.db_to_linear] and [method @GlobalScope.linear_to_db]. Alternatively, use [member volume_linear].

Volume of the stream, as a linear value. [b]Note:[/b] This member modifies [member volume_db] for convenience. The returned value is equivalent to the result of [method @GlobalScope.db_to_linear] on [member volume_db]. Setting this member is equivalent to setting [member volume_db] to the result of [method @GlobalScope.linear_to_db] on a value.

signal.name: finished
signal.description: Emitted when the stream finishes playing without interruptions, meaning this signal is [i]not[/i] emitted when calling [method stop], or when exiting the tree while the stream is playing. This signal is also not emitted if the [AudioStream] resource has looping enabled.

constants.constant: The audio will be played only on the first channel. This is the default.
The audio will be played on all surround channels.
The audio will be played on the second channel, which is usually the center.
